top_signals.title: Mortgage Rates Hit Highest Since August as War Fans Inflation
top_signals.summary: Diane Swonk, Chief Economist at KPMG joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." US mortgage rates surged this week to the highest level since August, threatening a spring selling season that was just starting to get a pulse. The average rate for 30-year fixed loans jumped to 6.51% from 6.36%, the biggest weekly increase since the end of March, Freddie Mac reported on Thursday.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Former EF developer Dankrad Feist suggests $1 billion raise to form new Ethereum advocacy group
top_signals.summary: The Ethereum Foundation is a non-profit stewarding the development of the world's second-largest blockchain.
